message:
chore(beads): close ft-lwa5q — finding no longer applies post-9bbb2a60

cc6 review filed ft-lwa5q on 2026-04-26 noting that
crates/frankenterm-core-replay/ was a dead stub post the baef663e
revert: lib.rs declared 28 mods with none on disk, and the 28
replay_*.rs files were untracked in crates/frankenterm-core/src/.

The follow-up extraction landed at 9bbb2a60 (ft-j1qjt.2): 24 of the 28
modules now physically reside in crates/frankenterm-core-replay/src/
with a Cargo.toml carrying the right deps (serde, serde_json,
thiserror, tracing, chrono, sha2, flate2, toml, hex, frankenterm-core,
frankenterm-core-replay-types) and a lib.rs that declares exactly the
24 moved modules. The 3 bridge modules (replay, replay_capture,
replay_fixture_harvest) intentionally stay in core because policy /
runtime / workflows / recorder_replay reach into them; the 28th
(replay_decision_graph) was already in frankenterm-core-replay-types
from ft-j1qjt.1.

Verified: cargo check -p frankenterm-core-replay clean; lib.rs has 24
pub mod declarations matching 24 .rs files on disk; sub-crate is a
real workspace member, not a stub.

Closes ft-lwa5q.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-8wt4o","title":"[gap] README runtime_compat quarantine claim not matched by frankenterm-core product paths","description":"README.md:41 claims only the remaining watch-daemon/runtime startup path still crosses `runtime_compat`; grep shows live product modules still depend on `runtime_compat` in crates/frankenterm-core/src/snapshot_engine.rs:37, crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs:5918, crates/frankenterm-core/src/event_stream.rs:599, crates/frankenterm-core/src/distributed.rs:1037, and crates/frankenterm-core/src/vendored/mux_client.rs:15,308.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:34:15.760863Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T04:19:01.893772Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T18:02:27.754618Z","close_reason":"Fixed in af925d26 — README runtime_compat boundary claim corrected to acknowledge multiple production seams","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs"]}
 {"id":"ft-8ybme","title":"[scan_pipeline] process_chunk allows append-after-flush-pending growth","description":"ChunkedPipelineState::process_chunk only exposes should_flush() as an advisory signal. In crates/frankenterm-core/src/scan_pipeline.rs:221-223 and 450-516, callers can keep appending after the active raw-data buffer has already crossed max_buffer_bytes, so a stalled or forgotten flusher grows trigger_data_buffer/uncompressed_buffer without bound.

Repro:
1. let pipeline = ScanPipeline::default();
2. let mut state = ChunkedPipelineState::new(64);
3. pipeline.process_chunk(&vec![b'x'; 80], &mut state);
4. state.should_flush() == true
5. pipeline.process_chunk(&vec![b'y'; 80], &mut state);
6. raw replay buffer keeps growing instead of failing fast or forcing a flush.

Expected: once flush is pending, a subsequent non-empty process_chunk call should refuse to accumulate more bytes rather than silently converting backpressure into unbounded memory growth.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:55:00.532379Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T22:00:57.219221Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T22:00:57.218185Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 658b945a: scan_pipeline now fails fast when flush is pending","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-94juo","title":"[modes-of-reasoning] no CI guard against new sub-crate cycles — cargo cycles caught 3x during ft-y0loj extractions; needs pre-flight check","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-26T06:46:24.506909Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T06:46:24.506909Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-9d4a3","title":"[MEDIUM] EventBus stats overreport queued backlog after subscribers drain channel","description":"Review finding in crates/frankenterm-core/src/events.rs:655-674 and 1942-1970. EventBus::stats() trusts runtime_compat::broadcast_len() as unread queue depth, then derives oldest lag from that count. Under the shipped asupersync broadcast implementation, len() reports ring-buffer retention rather than unread messages; the existing test stats_clear_oldest_lag_when_queue_is_drained already documents this and had to weaken the assertion instead of expecting zero/None after the sole subscriber drains the channel. Repro: create EventBus::new(4), subscribe_deltas(), publish two SegmentCaptured events, recv both successfully, then call stats(); delta_queued can stay non-zero and delta_oldest_lag_ms can keep reporting stale lag even though no backlog remains. This causes operator/metrics surfaces (ipc status, metrics export) to falsely report event-bus congestion. Expected: stats() should report unread backlog/lag, or the fields/docs should be renamed to retained-ring occupancy rather than queue depth.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T18:14:13.498770Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T19:16:04.232903Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T19:16:04.232646Z","close_reason":"Track unread backlog from slowest subscriber instead of retained ring occupancy","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["patterns","review"]}
 {"id":"ft-9unrg","title":"frankenterm-core no-default-features fails: ars_drift tests miss DriftMonitor import","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:09:49.756811Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:08:46.370803Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T18:08:46.370517Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-9xbxe","title":"mcp_tools test helper hits asupersync vs fastmcp_rust::asupersync version conflict","description":"## Defect

\`crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs:4812\` cannot compile under
\`--features mcp\`:

\`\`\`
error[E0308]: mismatched types
    --> crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs:4812:25
     |
4812 |         McpContext::new(asupersync::Cx::for_testing(), 1)
     |         --------------- ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
     |                         expected \`fastmcp_rust::Cx\`,
     |                         found \`asupersync::Cx\`
     |
note: there are multiple different versions of crate \`asupersync\` in
      the dependency graph
\`\`\`

\`McpContext::new\` (defined in \`fastmcp_rust\`) expects
\`fastmcp_rust::Cx\` (i.e. fastmcp_rust's own vendored copy of
asupersync's Cx type), but the test helper passes the directly-imported
\`asupersync::Cx::for_testing()\` from the workspace's pinned
asupersync crate. With version drift between fastmcp_rust's vendored
asupersync (0.2.6 per the older fastmcp_rust pin) and the workspace
asupersync (0.2.9 per /Users/jemanuel/.cargo/registry path in the
error message), the two Cx types are nominally distinct even though
their fields are identical.

## Concrete failure path

1. Run \`cargo test --no-run --lib -p frankenterm-core --features mcp\`.
2. Compiler tries to build the \`#[cfg(feature = "mcp")]\` test module
   in mcp_tools.rs.
3. \`test_mcp_context()\` at line 4811-4813 fails to type-check because
   \`McpContext::new\` from fastmcp_rust expects \`fastmcp_rust::Cx\` but
   the call site passes \`asupersync::Cx\` from the workspace's
   asupersync.
4. **Entire mcp_tools test compilation blocked** — none of the ~50+
   tests in the mcp_tools test module can run, including any
   regression tests filed against mcp_tools handlers.

## Reproduction

\`\`\`
CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang \\
CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ \\
CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rv-p0-target \\
cargo test --no-run --lib -p frankenterm-core \\
  --no-default-features --features mcp 2>&1 | grep -B2 -A10 E0308
\`\`\`

## Severity rationale

- Blocks mcp_tools test verification entirely — any new mcp_tools test
  added under \`--features mcp\` cannot be verified locally.
- Real defect-finding work in the mcp surface (e.g. ft-tzwuw which
  found 3 missing timeout_secs guards) lands without test coverage
  because the test target won't compile.
- Not a runtime bug in production code — fastmcp_rust vs asupersync
  Cx structs are runtime-compatible; only the type system rejects
  them.

## Fix sketch

One of:
1. Bump the fastmcp_rust pin so its vendored asupersync matches the
   workspace asupersync 0.2.9 (preferred — single source of truth).
2. Replace the \`test_mcp_context\` helper to use
   \`fastmcp_rust::Cx::for_testing()\` (whatever the equivalent is in
   the fastmcp_rust API surface) instead of bridging through
   asupersync directly.
3. Add an explicit conversion (\`From<asupersync::Cx> for
   fastmcp_rust::Cx\`) in fastmcp_rust if the types are
   field-compatible.

(1) is the cleanest because it eliminates the duplicated dependency.
(2) is the smallest local change.

## Scope

Diagnostic only — fix likely lives in fastmcp_rust pin (Cargo.toml)
or the test helper. Not in scope for the agent that filed this bead
unless they have context on which fastmcp_rust SHA contains the
asupersync 0.2.9 alignment.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:52:59.938463Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:12:26.989722Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T18:12:26.989533Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
@@ -218,6 +219,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-fs42k","title":"connector_outbound_bridge: dedup uses event.timestamp_ms (parallel ft-dijpe miss)","description":"## Defect

\`crates/frankenterm-core/src/connector_outbound_bridge.rs:1027\` passes
\`event.timestamp_ms\` (caller-supplied via the public
\`OutboundEvent::with_timestamp_ms\` builder, line 284) to
\`check_and_record\` as the dedup eviction clock — the exact shape that
ft-dijpe just fixed in the inbound bridge.

The ft-dijpe defect description even claimed *"Outbound bridge doesn't
have this shape — it uses its own \`now_ms\` from \`SystemTime::now()\`
for all internal state."* That claim is wrong. The outbound bridge's
constructor (line 245) does use \`SystemTime::now()\` to seed
\`timestamp_ms\` by default, but:

1. The public \`with_timestamp_ms\` builder (line 284) lets any caller
   override that default to an arbitrary value.
2. \`route_event\` line 1027 then passes that caller-controlled value
   directly to \`OutboundDeduplicator::check_and_record\`, which calls
   \`evict_expired(now_ms)\` and pops anything whose
   \`now_ms - inserted_at_ms > ttl_ms\`.
3. Same replay-bypass attack as ft-dijpe: a caller submitting two
   copies of the same correlation_id with the second copy's
   \`timestamp_ms = ttl_ms + 1\` triggers eviction of the first entry
   and the replay is treated as new.

## Concrete failure path

1. Internal caller (workflow handler, retry loop, test harness) builds
   an \`OutboundEvent\` and calls \`with_timestamp_ms\` to override the
   default \`SystemTime::now()\` (legitimate reason: replaying historical
   events for backfill, fixture-driven tests, or a buggy clock-advance
   helper).
2. First dispatch with \`correlation_id=X, timestamp_ms=0\`.
3. Second dispatch with \`correlation_id=X, timestamp_ms=ttl_ms+1\`.
4. \`evict_expired\` sees \`now_ms=ttl+1 > inserted_at=0+ttl\` → pops X.
5. \`check_and_record\` finds cache empty, inserts a new X, returns
   \`true\` → second dispatch routes as if new. All downstream
   side-effects fire twice (slack post, JIRA ticket, GitHub PR
   comment, credential broker call, etc.).

Trust model is weaker than inbound's — outbound events are
internally constructed rather than attacker-supplied — but the
defense-in-depth argument applies. A buggy internal caller that
mis-sets \`timestamp_ms\` should not silently bypass dedup, and
symmetry with the inbound bridge's hardening makes the codebase
easier to reason about.

## Severity rationale

- Same shape as ft-dijpe (silent dedup bypass), one severity level
  lower because the trust model is internal-only.
- Reachable today via any internal caller misusing
  \`with_timestamp_ms\`. Future caller bugs are likelier than
  attacker reach.

## Fix sketch

Mirror the ft-dijpe fix: in \`route_event\` line 1027, compute
\`now_ms\` from \`SystemTime::now().duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH).as_millis()\`
and pass that to \`check_and_record\` instead of \`event.timestamp_ms\`.
The \`event.timestamp_ms\` field stays useful as an analytics signal
(end-to-end source→sink latency, dispatch-history correlation) but
must NOT determine when dedup entries expire.

## Regression test

\`bridge_dedup_ignores_event_timestamp_ms_for_eviction\`: dispatch X
with \`correlation_id='r', timestamp_ms=0\`, then dispatch X with
\`correlation_id='r', timestamp_ms=u64::MAX/2\`. Assert the second is
\`deduplicated=true\`. Pre-fix the second returns \`deduplicated=false\`
because evict_expired with the spoofed future-timestamp pops the live
entry.

## Scope

\`crates/frankenterm-core/src/connector_outbound_bridge.rs\` only.
The line-1080 \`let now_ms = event.timestamp_ms\` (used downstream for
\`created_at_ms\`, \`broker_checked_at_ms\`, \`dispatched_at_ms\`) is a
separate concern — those telemetry timestamps arguably should also
be SystemTime::now() but that's a wider change. This bead covers
ONLY the dedup eviction clock.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane0","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:25:28.629996Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:26:19.140282Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:26:19.139925Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of ft-ptjlp (same defect, same line, parallel filing race). Sibling agent already applied the SystemTime::now() fix in working tree at connector_outbound_bridge.rs:1037-1044 with their own ft-ptjlp tag in the inline comment. Closing this bead so ft-ptjlp owns the fix and the test work. Reviewed their fix — it's identical to what I would have written (SystemTime::now() → dedup_now_ms → check_and_record), correctly preserves event.timestamp_ms for downstream telemetry uses.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-fvt0b","title":"codec oversized-container regression overconstrains error wording","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:43:12.478186Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:50:17.315201Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:50:17.314833Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 4d6c4abc; verified with codec --lib and conformance_pdu_wire","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-fy44g","title":"[review][note] reviewer audit converged — 5 consecutive rounds with no substantive defects filed; work has saturated on polish","description":"Formal convergence signal at end of continuous review session. 109 commits audited across 27+ review rounds. Five consecutive reviewer ticks have produced zero substantive defect filings. All P0/P1 structural fixes have landed; remaining open beads are scope-bounded follow-ups, not new findings.

## Tick-level convergence evidence

- Round 25 (f540a5d5 shared rate limiter + ddf4eb8f drift catch + 0f744567 TOON tx): 0 beads
- Round 26 (ft-siwlu sanity + 97016fe6 + 7d6fdac8 11-of-12 deny audit): 0 beads
- Round 27 (d9d7d361 redactor extract + 1ada5ffe coord close): 0 beads
- Final rollup (107 commits): 0 beads
- This tick (65ea21d5 approval hints + a94abb0e AGENTS.md docs): 0 beads

## Recent commits are clearly polish

- Approval recovery hints (actionable strings pointing at ft watch and ft approve CODE)
- AGENTS.md module-map rows for the newly-landed redactor module and policy-denial audit stream
- Re-export-based module extraction with zero downstream breakage
- Coordination concerns resolving naturally without the predicted rebase storm

## Open bead backlog owned cleanly

- ft-eu0no HIGH: structural fix shipped f540a5d5 + ddf4eb8f; test execution tracked as ft-eu0no.1
- ft-2zbie MEDIUM: pane 4 TOON regeneration in flight, 9 placeholder goldens to close naturally
- ft-xydxz + ft-rpjud LOW: ftvi fuzz corpus-quality improvements (non-blocking)

## Next tick posture

Per user instruction the next pane 6 tick is targeted-audit only. This convergence note is the handoff signal: stop continuous watch, resume only on specific commit/bead target.

## Systemic state

Session-start vs session-end deltas already rolled up in the 'Final Reviewer Rollup' narrative. Security posture: MCP rate limiting / authorization / deny audit / read-path redaction all moved from patchy-or-absent to comprehensively gated. Correctness posture: tx compensation, mission dispatch, cancel propagation all moved from synthetic/fake to verified end-to-end.

No new concern. Filed as the formal convergence artifact per user request. Tag: review, convergence, session-end.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T21:49:22.012583Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T22:00:37.307563Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T22:00:37.307014Z","close_reason":"Reviewer audit converged; documented in note","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}
+{"id":"ft-fytns","title":"[modes-of-reasoning] sub-crate publish ordering undocumented — leaf crates must publish before frankenterm-core; release tooling needs to know","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-26T06:46:24.806491Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T06:46:24.806491Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-g3g1c","title":"[review][MEDIUM] release.yml pins actions/attest to mutable @v4 tag","description":"release.yml:145 uses actions/attest@v4 — mutable major-version alias. Verified resolves to v4.1.0 today via GitHub releases API. actions/attest default mode (no sbomPath/predicate) IS SLSA provenance per src/detect.ts, so functionally correct. Concern: attestation action pinning by moving tag undermines its own supply-chain purpose. Fix: pin to 40-char SHA. Audit round 6.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:32:27.714567Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:35:06.046980Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T18:35:06.046712Z","close_reason":"Landed in eba16ce3: pinned actions/attest to immutable SHA 59d89421af93a897026c735860bf21b6eb4f7b26 (v4.1.0).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}
 {"id":"ft-g3z31","title":"[review][LOW] ft-ojgmo lacks regression test for pre-cancel fast-path in EventSubscriber::recv_cx","description":"fa1fbffe added two cancel-detection paths to EventSubscriber::recv_cx at crates/frankenterm-core/src/events.rs:789-817:

1. Pre-cancel fast-path at :793:
       if cx.is_cancel_requested() { return Err(RecvError::Cancelled); }
2. Mid-flight cancel watcher at :798-806 (select race with 50ms polling).

The new regression test `event_subscriber_recv_cx_surfaces_mid_flight_cancel` at :1573-1601 exercises the MID-FLIGHT path only — cancel is signalled 100ms AFTER recv_cx is entered. The PRE-CANCEL fast-path (cx already cancelled when recv_cx is called) is untested at the EventSubscriber layer.

Evidence: search for calls to EventSubscriber::recv_cx with a pre-cancelled cx in the test suite — none exist. The runtime_compat.rs test at :6189 tests pre-cancel on the lower-level broadcast_recv_with_cx primitive, but not the EventSubscriber wrapper that is the public API.

Why it matters: the pre-cancel shortcut is one line, but it was the difference between a hot loop of polling wakeups and immediate return. A refactor that moves the cancel check inside the select! block (losing the shortcut) would regress the behavior silently — the mid-flight test would still pass because the 50ms poll would eventually fire.

Fix: add a sibling test:

    #[test]
    fn event_subscriber_recv_cx_surfaces_pre_cancel_immediately() {
        run_async_test(async {
            let cx = crate::cx::Cx::for_testing();
            cx.cancel_with(crate::outcome::CancelKind::User, Some("pre-cancel"));
            let bus = EventBus::new(8);
            let mut sub = bus.subscribe();
            let started = Instant::now();
            let result = sub.recv_cx(&cx).await;
            assert!(matches!(result, Err(RecvError::Cancelled)));
            assert!(started.elapsed() < Duration::from_millis(5),
                "pre-cancel must return immediately, took {:?}", started.elapsed());
        });
    }

A <5ms assertion distinguishes the shortcut path from the 50ms poll path.

Severity: LOW — not a correctness bug, just a coverage gap. Discovered during reviewer audit round 11, 2026-04-23 while verifying fa1fbffe.

Tag: review, events, cancellation, test-coverage.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:07:57.398125Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:20:12.679034Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:20:12.678785Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 8ae03256: add EventSubscriber::recv_cx pre-cancel regression test that asserts immediate Err(Cancelled) before any emission.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}
 {"id":"ft-g43fq","title":"[ft-7iof6.1] rename runtime_compat → runtime_async with one-release deprecated re-export","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-26T01:21:26.173200Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T03:13:51.947751Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T03:13:51.947464Z","close_reason":"0f15e1bc renames the canonical async surface module and its surface_guard sibling, with a one-release deprecated alias preserving 187 files / 1622 references. Same shape as cc7's ft-zoxxq.1 (8bf2a272) WeztermInterface→MuxInterface rename. Concrete changes: (1) git mv runtime_compat.rs → runtime_async.rs (clean rename, 100% similar), (2) git mv runtime_compat_surface_guard.rs → runtime_async_surface_guard.rs (98% similar — only the one internal RAW_TOKIO_RUNTIME_BUILDER_QUARANTINE_V1 path updated), (3) lib.rs replaces the two pub mod declarations and adds #[deprecated] pub use … as old_name aliases for both modules so callers keep compiling, (4) AGENTS.md updated in 4 places to reflect the rename has landed. Compile-time alias works: every existing crate::runtime_compat::Foo resolves through the alias to runtime_async::Foo. The #[deprecated] note doesn't propagate through pub-use module aliases (known Rust gotcha, same as cc7's WeztermInterface alias) — call-site sweep is left for a follow-up bead. cargo check --workspace --all-targets clean via rch on /tmp/ft-cc6-target. Did not touch codec/ or frankenterm/client/ (cc7 lane).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","refactor","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-g43fq","depends_on_id":"ft-7iof6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-26T01:21:31.145568Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -301,7 +303,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-lnlg4","title":"[reality-gap] tui/ftui_stub.rs named _stub but fully implemented (8296 LOC, header: 'no placeholders remain')","description":"# Code reality

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/tui/ftui_stub.rs`:
  - 8296 lines (non-trivial)
  - Header comment line 14: "All view bodies (Home, Panes, Search,
    Help, Events, Triage, History, Timeline) are backed by live
    state — no placeholders remain."
  - `crates/frankenterm-core/src/tui/mod.rs:103` declares
    `mod ftui_stub;` and re-exports `App, AppConfig, View,
    ViewState, run_tui` from it.

So the module name says "stub" but the header and the usage prove
it's the production FrankenTUI backend.

# Why this is a reality-check gap

Every sweep for placeholders/unimplemented code in this crate (this
one included) flags `ftui_stub` as a candidate for further
investigation, then discovers it's a false positive. Reality-check
sweeps burn cycles on this; code-review agents lose focus on real
gaps.

The naming is a legacy from when the file genuinely was a stub. The
content has grown into the real backend. The file name didn't
follow.

# Recommended disposition

```bash
git mv crates/frankenterm-core/src/tui/ftui_stub.rs \
       crates/frankenterm-core/src/tui/ftui_backend.rs
```

…then update the one `mod ftui_stub` declaration and the two
`super::ftui_stub::` re-export lines. 15-second edit, preserves
git history via `git mv`.

# Risk

- `git mv` preserves blame + history.
- No external dependency on the file name (it's a sibling module).
- Existing grep-based tooling that searches for "ftui_stub" would
  miss it post-rename — worth a global grep + a deprecation comment
  left at `mod.rs` during the rename window.

# Severity

Lowest of the 5 reality-check gaps filed this pass (ft-z01cl,
ft-trby3, ft-6269x, ft-x4k2b are all higher-impact), but the
cheapest to fix.  {"id":"ft-lokam","title":"[LOW] diag bundle environment.json leaks absolute workspace cwd; tracing fields leak operator paths","description":"Found during /security-audit-for-saas pass #6 (pane 5, 2026-04-21).

## Summary
Three logging/telemetry surfaces all come out clean on the obvious
secret-leak vectors (no bearer-token / API-key / pane-content
fields in tracing calls; no /etc/ or .ssh/ reads in diag
collection). One defense-in-depth gap remains: path privacy.

## Findings

### F1 [LOW] diag bundle environment.json includes absolute cwd
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/diagnostic.rs:87-89
  ```rust
  cwd: std::env::current_dir()
      .ok()
      .map(|p| p.display().to_string()),
  ```
- Diag bundles (`ft diag bundle`) are often attached to bug reports
  and shared outside the host. An absolute cwd reveals:
  - the user's home path segment (`/Users/<username>/...` or
    `/home/<username>/...`),
  - the project path (`/Users/alice/projects/secret-startup-pitch-deck/`),
  - on some setups, mount points that hint at filesystem layout.
- Severity: LOW. Users implicitly share the workspace when
  debugging, but the current behavior removes the choice.

### F2 [LOW] tracing fields log operator-private paths
- Multiple `tracing::[info|warn|debug]!(..., path = %X.display(), ...)`
  call sites scatter socket paths, DB paths, and workspace paths
  through structured logs. Representative hits (non-exhaustive):
    - crates/frankenterm-core/src/native_events.rs:258, 302, 331,
      362, 401 — unix socket paths.
    - crates/frankenterm-core/src/telemetry.rs:1118 —
      `info_span!("telemetry_store_open", path = %db_path.display())`.
    - crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime.rs:1375, 2658, 2814 —
      socket paths on accept failures.
    - crates/frankenterm-core/src/build_coord.rs:265 —
      `meta_path = %self.meta_path.display()`.
- Severity: LOW. These logs land in the user's local log file,
  not network-visible by default, but they do end up in diag
  bundles when `collect_recent_log_lines` is called.

## Non-findings verified in the same pass
- Bearer tokens in structured logs: grep across
  `tracing::{info,debug,warn,error}!` call sites for
  `token|bearer|secret|password|api_key` returned zero production
  hits. Clean.
- Pane content in tracing fields: no production code logs
  `segment.content`, `delta_text`, or `pane_text`. Only test
  `assert_eq!(segment.content, ...)` references found. Clean.
- Diag bundle reading privileged files: no `/etc/`, `.ssh/`,
  `.aws/`, `.gnupg/` reads in `diagnostic.rs`. The bundle only
  reads the ft SQLite DB (already redacted via `redact_events`)
  and writes to the workspace-local `diag_dir`. Clean.
- Environment variables in diag bundle: `gather_environment`
  captures only OS / arch / rust_version / wa_version / cwd —
  no `std::env::vars()` dump. Clean.
- Summarize_config: the docstring already calls out "no paths or
  secrets" and the implementation confirms — only numeric/boolean
  config values are copied over. Clean.

## Fix direction
- F1: replace absolute cwd with just the basename, or gate
  inclusion behind `DiagnosticOptions.include_cwd: bool` default
  `false`. Concretely at diagnostic.rs:87:
  ```rust
  cwd: if opts.include_cwd {
      std::env::current_dir().ok().map(|p| p.display().to_string())
  } else {
      std::env::current_dir()
          .ok()
          .and_then(|p| p.file_name().map(|n| n.to_string_lossy().into_owned()))
  },
  ```
- F2: when a log line is included in a diag bundle via
  `collect_recent_log_lines` (or equivalent), run it through the
  existing `Redactor` path-aware rules. Non-bundle in-process logs
  stay as-is since they aid operator debugging on the local box.

## Severity rationale
- LOW (P2): neither finding reveals a secret, and both sit behind
  an operator action (`ft diag bundle` + sharing it). The fix
  shrinks the unintentional-share footprint but doesn't close a
  live exploit.

## Related
- ft-ybtyg (598a7c13) — snapshot/checkpoint hashing honesty.
- ft-2guux (doc reality-check) — complementary; this bead is the
  diagnostics-exfil counterpart.

Filed per /security-audit-for-saas pass #6.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T04:48:38.534325Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T04:53:28.901879Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T04:53:28.901341Z","close_reason":"Closed by e790f481. F1 (cwd leak) fixed: DiagnosticOptions gains include_full_cwd:bool (default false). gather_environment now routes through new scrub_path_to_basename helper which returns only the final path component; opt-in via include_full_cwd:true restores absolute path for legitimate mount-point/multi-host repro cases. Both production entry points (generate_bundle + generate_bundle_with_cx) forward opts. 8 regression tests cover basename-only default, root/empty fallback to <scrubbed> sentinel, relative paths, opt-in restore, and the strongest guarantee: default environment.json output cannot contain /Users/ or /home/ segments. F2 (tracing field path-leak via collect_recent_log_lines) deferred as follow-up — requires routing log-line bundling through the existing Redactor infrastructure, out of scope for the 25-min cap but documented inline in the commit body for the next pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","diagnostics","logging","privacy","security"]}
 {"id":"ft-ltpog","title":"native_events: decode errors logged at debug, invisible in production","description":"# [MEDIUM] native_events: malformed wire events logged at debug

**Subsystem:** native_events / protocol_recovery
**File:** crates/frankenterm-core/src/native_events.rs (`handle_connection_with_cx`)

## The hole

When wire-event decoding fails (invalid JSON, bad base64, unknown event
type), the handler emitted:

\`\`\`rust
Err(err) => {
    debug!(error = %err, "failed to decode native event (cx path)");
}
\`\`\`

A malformed wire event is a protocol anomaly — version skew between
frankenterm and the mux-server, on-wire corruption, or a hostile peer
writing into the native-events socket. Operators routinely filter
`debug!` in production, so the signal was effectively lost.

Contrast with the `MAX_EVENT_LINE_BYTES` path a few lines above, which
already emits at `warn!`. Same class of anomaly, inconsistent level.

## Fix

Promoted the decode-error log to `warn!`. The `warn!` import was already
present — no dependency change.

Future work (separate bead): attach a telemetry counter for
decode-failure rate so it can drive a SLI rather than relying solely on
log sampling.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:26:39.950825Z","created_by":"p3-review-audit","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:46:57.568904Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:46:57.568595Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["native-events","observability"]}

 {"id":"ft-m1zkw","title":"[MEDIUM] mux PaneNode serde proptests omit the recursive Split variant","description":"Audit finding from commits 095ca88a + ccaa7385.

Files:
- frankenterm/mux/tests/proptest_tab_wire_serde.rs:108-113
- frankenterm/mux/src/tab.rs:3907-3914

`PaneNode` is a recursive wire type used directly by the codec and it has three variants: `Empty`, `Leaf`, and `Split { left, right, node }`. The current generator only produces `Empty` and `Leaf`:

```rust
fn arb_pane_node() -> impl Strategy<Value = PaneNode> {
    prop_oneof![
        Just(PaneNode::Empty),
        arb_pane_entry().prop_map(PaneNode::Leaf),
    ]
}
```

So `pane_node_json_roundtrip` never exercises the recursive split-tree encoding path. The follow-up commit ccaa7385 added scalar roundtrips for `SplitDirection` and `SplitSize`, but that still does not cover nested `PaneNode::Split` layouts or the interaction with `SplitDirectionAndSize`.

Expected fix:
- make `arb_pane_node()` recursive and generate bounded split trees
- assert roundtrip for at least one non-trivial nested split shape

Regression risk: codec-facing tab topology serde can regress on split trees while the current proptests stay green.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:49:43.315765Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T18:12:27.500875Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T18:12:27.500567Z","close_reason":"Already fixed by fef393d1 (ft-n38nt) — arb_pane_node() now uses prop_recursive(3, 16, 2) generating bounded split trees, plus nested split shape fixture test","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["codec","mux","review","serde","test-gap"]}
 {"id":"ft-m9gob","title":"robot_envelope::canonicalize_json panics on huge non-integer f64 via (float * 10000) overflow to infinity","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/robot_envelope.rs:48-57`:

```rust
Value::Number(number) => {
    if let Some(float) = number.as_f64() {
        if float.fract() != 0.0 {
            let rounded = (float * 10_000.0).round() / 10_000.0;
            *value = Value::Number(
                serde_json::Number::from_f64(rounded).expect("rounded float"),
            );
        }
    }
}
```

For any non-integer f64 where `|float|` is above roughly `1.8e304`, the multiplication `float * 10_000.0` overflows to `f64::INFINITY`. `Infinity.round() / 10_000.0` remains Infinity. `serde_json::Number::from_f64(Infinity)` returns `None` (JSON disallows non-finite numbers). The unconditional `.expect("rounded float")` then PANICS the caller.

## Concrete trigger

A robot envelope payload that deserializes a number like `1e305` (valid JSON, fits in f64, finite) — e.g., a bridge that surfaces a cosmic-scale metric, a misconfigured tuning value, or an adversarial payload — passes through `canonicalize_json` on its way to the golden-file path. `1e305 * 10_000 = 1e309` → INFINITY → panic.

The bug is reachable:
1. From trusted internal bridges that happen to emit large-but-finite f64 values.
2. From any test-fixture or dev tool that round-trips JSON through canonicalize_json without sanitizing numeric ranges.

## Fix

Two options, both safe:
1. Short-circuit: `if !float.is_finite() { return; }` at the top of the Number arm. The caller's original (finite but huge) value is preserved — no canonicalization but no panic.
2. Fallback: replace `.expect` with a match that falls back to the original number on None. Same observable behavior, defense-in-depth against any future non-finite source.

Combine both: skip canonicalization when the ORIGINAL float is non-finite OR the ROUNDED product is non-finite. Otherwise pass through unchanged (so the Value keeps the caller's original number, just un-rounded).

## Tests

- `canonicalize_json_preserves_huge_finite_float_without_panicking`: wrap a Value with a 1e305 float under a generic payload, call canonicalize_json, assert it returns without panic.
- `canonicalize_json_preserves_edge_floats`: f64::MAX as non-integer, a denormal value, verify no panic and the original finite value is preserved when the rounded value would overflow.

## Severity

MEDIUM — not a crash on the hot path, but canonicalize_json runs on every robot envelope through a golden-file comparator and every contract test. A single payload with a big f64 takes down the subprocess + anyone running the golden tests. Fix is 3 lines.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:51:09.506491Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:55:15.285120Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:55:15.284826Z","close_reason":"Landed in f93753e3 (fix(robot_envelope): guard canonicalize_json against huge-float rounding overflow)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
